headline: American Airlines to use SpaceX's Starlink, joining United & JetBlue
summary: American Airlines (AAL) is set to install SpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) Starlink on 500 of its aircraft in 2027. Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian breaks down the details above.

content.title: American Airlines to use SpaceX's Starlink, joining United & JetBlue
content.description:
American Airlines (AAL) is set to install SpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) Starlink on 500 of its aircraft in 2027.

Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian breaks down the details above.

headline: Oil Prices Fall, Despite Iran's 14-Point 'Fabrication'; Airline Stocks Fly
summary: Oil prices retreated close to $90 a barrel as markets bet that a U.S.-Iran deal is likely happen soon.

content.title: Oil Prices Fall, Despite Iran's 14-Point 'Fabrication'; Airline Stocks Fly
content.summary: Oil prices retreated close to $90 a barrel as markets bet that a U.S.-Iran deal is likely happen soon.

headline: Airline stocks rally as oil retreat eases fuel cost pressure
summary: Oil is down 8% over the last week as reports circulated that Washington and Tehran were edging closer to an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. That drop helped push the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record closing highs on Tuesday, underscoring the inverse relationship between crude prices and risk assets that has defined markets since the conflict began.

content.title: Airline stocks rally as oil retreat eases fuel cost pressure
content.summary: Oil is down 8% over the last week as reports circulated that Washington and Tehran were edging closer to an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. That drop helped push the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record closing highs on Tuesday, underscoring the inverse relationship between crude prices and risk assets that has defined markets since the conflict began.

headline: United and Other Airline Stocks Are Flying. There Are More Gains to Come.
summary: United, Delta and other airline stocks were flying early Wednesday as the sector finally put the Iran war in its wake.  The  exchange-traded fund, which tracks the performance of airline stocks, jumped 3.3% in early trading and was on track to close above its pre-war, Feb. 27 level for the first time since the conflict started.  United Airlines  was leading the sector higher, rising 6%, entering positive territory for the year and also on track for its first close above its Feb. 27 price since the war began.

headline: Is Delta (DAL) Using Event-Driven Routes to Quietly Redefine Its Global Competitive Strategy?
summary: Delta Air Lines recently expanded its trans-Atlantic schedule for November 2026 international football matches, added extra Madrid and Munich flights, extended its Atlanta–Tel Aviv suspension to November 2026, and was highlighted by analysts for relatively better fuel cost insulation amid strong sector sentiment. At the same time, Delta’s top-tier placement in U.S. Airline Rankings and routine insider stock sales underscore a business balancing robust customer demand, operational...

headline: Wall Street hangs near its records as oil prices ease
summary: U.S. stocks are hanging near their records Wednesday as oil prices fall and ease the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.  Delta Air Lines rose 4.1% and is on track to set an all-time high.

headline: US stocks inch to more records after oil prices drop
summary: U.S. stocks inched to more records Wednesday after oil prices fell and eased the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 182 points, or 0.4%, and the Nasdaq composite gained 0.1% as both indexes also set records.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.
